title: marsilea costulifera
intro: Marsilea costulifera, also known as water clover, is a type of aquatic plant that belongs to the family Marsileaceae. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery. The plant's leaves and stems contain compounds that have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, offering potential health benefits in reducing inflammation and preventing infections. Marsilea costulifera is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and can also be used in combination with other herbs in traditional medicine to create remedies for various health conditions.
